entry.title: Re: DDMRP prioritized share
entry.content: Thanks Jan, so in this example, how will buffers be replenished using the supplier capacity (same supplier for all plants and all plants with buffer)?

entry.title: Excited to be a part of the community
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am a supply chain management student with a strong passion for pursuing a career in this dynamic and essential field. I am thrilled to join the Beginner's Corner and connect with like-minded individuals who share a similar enthusiasm for supply chain management. Looking forward to learning, sharing, and growing together in this exciting journey!

entry.title: Re: Lease-in accounting - migration
entry.content: Nothing as of yet still waiting on this. I have created a seperate blog for this https://community.sap.com/t5/finance-q-a/migration-of-lease-in-contract-in-s4-public-cloud/qaq-p/13748082/comment-id/9864#M9864

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2408 Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We’re thrilled to introduce several innovative features designed to enhance your procurement processes. These updates focus on improving visibility, efficiency, and integration across our SAP Ariba solutions. For those of you looking for a deeper dive into specific functionalities, we’ll also provide links to detailed blogs that explore a more comprehensive view for specific areas. Let's dive right in!

Master Data Overview in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (CIM-1428)

With the new Display Imported Master Data app, you can now view all master data along with replication details imported to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management from connected systems. This feature addresses the need for visibility over replicated master data, ensuring that you can validate and monitor data replication efficiently.

Extract Multiple PO References at Header Level (CIM-431)

Invoice processing just got a lot smoother. The Extract at Header Level Multiple PO References from Invoice feature utilizes SAP AI Business Services' Document Information Extraction to pull multiple PO reference values directly at the header level of an invoice. This advancement enhances automation and accuracy in handling invoices, helping you save time and reduce manual workload.

Duplicate Invoice Detection (CIM-220)

Enhance the reliability of your invoice processing automation with the Duplicate Invoice Detection feature. This function checks fields such as company code, invoicing party, external reference, and invoice date in draft invoices against existing invoices during editing or transfer. It automatically flags duplicates, preventing errors and potential fraud.

Purchase Requisition Approvals in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App (SAS-318)

Efficiency on the go! With the Support for Purchase Requisition Approvals in the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers can easily search for requisitions in their to-do list, view line-item details, and approve or reject them through a role-based user interface. This feature ensures that crucial approval workflows are accessible anytime, anywhere. Read more about how this feature can help streamline your mobile procurement processes in SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App The - Purchase Requisition Approvals screen

 

Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis (ARI-20913)

Gain critical insights into your suppliers' risks with the Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis feature. This tool allows you to view and analyze suppliers based on country/region and commodity risk, and easily export segmentation reports to a spreadsheet.

The Segmented View page with the new Create Engagement Request button

 

Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure (ARI-23844)

Customize your risk assessment with the Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure feature. You now have the flexibility to use your own source of country risk score information, rather than solely relying on default data. Tailor your risk exposure configurations to better align with your organization's specific criteria and needs. For more information on SAP Ariba Supplier Management features, take a look at the SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

Viewing Supplier Risk Level in SAP Ariba Category Management (CAT-5800)

Understanding supplier risk levels is vital for strategic category management. The new integration between SAP Ariba Category Management and SAP Ariba Supplier Risk allows category managers to view and assess supplier risk levels (Low, Medium, High). This visibility is essential for making informed decisions and strategizing effectively.

The new Risk Level available in the Category Suppliers page

Integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers (CAT-7893)

Enhance your cost structure analysis with the ability to integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers. Category management administrators can now incorporate external cost structure data directly into the Cost Structure tool within the strategy and plan document. This addition allows for more comprehensive and accurate strategy formulation. To delve deeper into this and other SAP Ariba Category Management, SAP Ariba guided sourcing, and SAP Ariba Contract Management, check out our SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

With these features, we are aiming to streamline your procurement processes, enhance risk management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. We encourage you to explore these exciting updates and implement them into your workflows to harness their full potential. Stay tuned for more updates by subscribing to myself and our other fantastic bloggers and take a look at the links below.

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2408 release
  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
  • Encourage customers to check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates.
  • Introduce customers to
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: Re: New PA is not visible V_001P_N
entry.content:
Hi,

make sure ctry Grpg is correctly filled in table T500P

entry.title: Re: Empty Adobe form when called from an application program
entry.content:
Hi Norbert,

Good morning. Thank you for your comments. I will take a look at this information.

Best regards,

Andres

entry.title: WTax Non-Subject Amount in Invoice Sap Business One
entry.content:
hi

i have one question, how does the WTax Non-Subject Amount field in the AP invoice table gets updated? we have several invoices that have zero amount, before that field got always populated

thanks for the support

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
I have been doing testing in our Development environment and I have tested the following scenarios that are not visible to the end user:

1. Item in curricula - flagged as Optional - No Due Date - doesn't show anywhere in new ILX

2. Item in curricula - flagged as Optional - WITH Due Date - doesn't show anywhere in new ILX

3. Requirement pool in curricula - No Due Date - doesn't show anywhere in new ILX

4. An item I added to a library 36 hours ago is still not visible in the "enhanced" search

5. Item search is not finding loads of items that have been in the library for months/years 

We really don't want to go live to our company with so many known issues, it's so embarrassing having to communicate to our employees that we have this new look but nothing works - can we get visibility ASAP of what is going to be fixed in 2h24 so we can decide if we should wait for the universal push? You really get one opportunity to sell big change to people and this is not going to land well.

How can SAP allow optional items in curricula but think it's OK to remove all visibility of these optional items? How can SAP call it an "Enhanced" search when legacy search is finding everything immediately and the new search isn't finding anything and takes hours to refresh.  Honestly the new experience looks much better but that is it, a pretty facade and the functionality is barely there.

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP Cloud ALM with JIRA
entry.content:
Great post,

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Is footer in fiori launchpad below the tiles possible?
entry.content: Thank you. This what i was looking for!

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow PO - How to Skip/Auto Approve
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you please let us know if your Agent Determination is based on BADI or you are using Manage teams and responsibilities?

Thanks,

entry.title: Data Validation in RAP web service with messages in response
entry.content:
Hi All,

I Have one scenario whare i have to validate some data using rap in odata web service. 

The data are coming in specific payload. I have to validate this data and if any error message is coming i have to send as response. This one is web Odata so i can not send messages as an error message. The all functionality i have to do in GET method only.

Any way to do this one?

sample payload

{
  "company_code" : "3N6R",
  "profit_center" : "",
  "trading_partner" : "",
  "gl_account" : "",
  "cost_center" : "",
  "internal_order" : "",
  "wbs_elements" : "",
  "Customer" : "",
  "Vendor" : "",
  "posting_date" : "\/Date(1721088000000)\/"
}

entry.title: Re: OCI version in our system
entry.content:
Hello,

You can capture an HTTP Trace using the HTTPWatch tool to get the OCI Version when calling a catalog from the corresponding SAP application.

Best regards, 
Tatiana

entry.title: Re: Inventory control for waffer/die in substrate fabrication

entry.title: Re: SAP BO 4.2 SP05: Windows Server OS Upgrade From 2008...

entry.title: Re: Inventory control for waffer/die in substrate fabrica...
entry.content: that mean next time it is 1 EA = 130 EA.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Yes Key Users can set Default Values for other Users
entry.content:
Thanks Jocelyn, Great blog, as always. 

Is there an advantage to doing Default Values the way you explain here, versus creating a public Variant  for the filter bar? Variants (or Views) are a more familiar and flexible option which don't require apps to have specific parameters. And I believe variants can be transported.

I am just curious which option I should recommend to my clients.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow PO - How to Skip/Auto Approve
entry.content: Planning to use BADI. Suggest me which is best option. In my Approval workflow level 1 is determined based Company code, Level 2 & 3 will determined based on Purchasing grp., level 4,5,&6 will be determined based on Total PO Amount.

entry.title: Saving the event reason to a custom field
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is anyone saving their event reason to a custom field on job or comp info? I know that event reason derivation rules trigger after on save rules, so simply having a business rule look up the event reason to save to a custom field will return a null value.

entry.title: Re: Create SAPUI5 multiple layout application
entry.content: I don't see a issue, you need to know how routing navigation works in ui5.

entry.title: Consecutive action for usage decision
entry.content:
Hello

I am looking for consecutive actions to configure on the usage decision in S/4HANA Cloud Public.

Is there a way to add consecutive actions like sending a notification or printing a document after the UD?

There is an existing MANUAL consecutive action, what is the purpose of it?

thank you

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow PO - How to Skip/Auto Approve
entry.content: I would suggest use BADI because you have many options to define. Also, it depends on your business requirements what they want. Coming to your current workflow where you mentioned Level 2 is the same user as Level 4 but the conditions are different. Can you please share screenshots?

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content:
Have been able to implement a productInfo function locally but, as usual, the deployment fails. cf deploy ends:

Application "northbreeze-srv" started and available at "....-dev-northbreeze-srv.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com"

The "Application Routes" displays a URL, which lists Service Endpoints however clicking on Products navigates to:

../odata/v4/northbreeze/Products

which only shows, <code>500</code><message>Internal Server Error</message>.

The log just says, "SqliteError: no such table: northbreeze_Products"

entry.title: Re: Data Validation in RAP web service with messages in r...
entry.content:
I am Getting error message like this one when service is triggered. Here Return code is 400. due to this system consider message as an error message.

But I want Message like this one. here Return code is 200. so on BTP i can read this message properly without any error.

entry.title: Re: Physical inventory in EWM for mass materials
entry.content: You can use transaction /SCWM/PI_COUNTLIST to count and post the results. If PI document is created for several materails and batches then counting and posting using this transaction is fairly easy and fast.

entry.title: Opendocument keeps looping in prompt parameter window
entry.content:
I use opendocument to open a report.

http://rapports-reports/BOE/OpenDocument/opendoc/openDocument.jsp?iDocID=78829&sType=rpt&sOutputFormat=P&sRefresh=Y&NAII=N&lsSpProp_No=7900&lsSpLanguage=en-ca

As you can see, I specify 2 parameters. There are 3 other parameters in the reports that are mandatory. So after clicking the link, BO display the prompt screen with the other 3 parameters. I select them and click "Run". But it keeps looping back to the prompt screen. Anyone had that problem? We only had this behaviour since yesterday when we upgraded to BO 4.3

entry.title: Re: Difference Between Asset Acquisition in F0859 and MIR...
entry.content: Please use F0859, the information has now been made available in the What's New for CE2408 that in Asset Accounting the catalog for MIRO will be removed with the F0859 being exclusively used in Asset Accounting, see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/f6c4933c19e54e9698e424ba2a6a82bf.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Be Authentic, Comfortable and Interview the Interviewer 🧑🏻‍💻
entry.content:
Kudos 😊

It's inspiring 🙌

entry.title: Re: RAP Unmanaged Query with MANAGED BO Problems
entry.content:
A managed rap BO is only supported with CDS views or better CDS view entities.   

Custom Entities can only be used with unmanaged RAP BO's.

entry.title: Batch session is not created in SM35 for few FCR runs
entry.content:
Hello All,

I need some help on session creation in SM35 for FCR run in SAP S4 HANA system.

I have an issue for the few FCR jobs the session is not created even the batch name is updated in the FCR input (T-code FAGL_FCV).

The input selection is same to create batch input session for all jobs but sessions is not created for few jobs. Hence could you please let me know what could be the reason for not generating in SM35.

Many thanks for your help and support.

Best Regards,

Noor Basha.

entry.title: Time off workflow tile is not visible to one group appro with same permission in Manage workflow gro
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Time off workflow tile is not visible to one group approver with same permission in Manage workflow group.

There is workflow group and in that two users are there. Both users are getting all EC approvals, but one user is not able to see time off approval requests where other is able to both approval tiles.

 

Regards,

Jyoti

entry.title: Re: How to pass error message from read method in behavio...
entry.content:
The requirement sounds weird to show an error message rather than using DCL to filter out such entries based on authorizations.   

If you really want to implement something like this in a read only app so called custom entities might be an option.

Here you are implementing the query via ABAP code.

How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTf... - SAP Community

Regards,

Andre

entry.title: Misalignment between start date and requirrements date for component
entry.content:
Good evening. I'm facing for a client a huge misalignement between the start date of the process order and the requirements date of components. There are two different order types: one for individual order and one for collective order. For both of them, I've already checked the OPU3, OPU5, and OPUZ, and the configuration is exactly the same for both of them. 

The behaviour for this two types of order is the same: 

  • Material components are linked to operation 0020.
  • The first operation that is performed in the process order is operation 0130.
  • Operation 0020 starts when 0130 is already finished.

But: 

  • for order type of collective orders, the requirrement dates of components is the same of op 0020 and so it is after the process order start date
  • for order type of individual orders , the requirrement dates of components is the same of the start date of the component

I've already check the scheduling capacity for work center and scheduling parameters but the configuration is practically the same. 

Would you please help me? 

Kind regards

entry.title: Error Logs in SQL SAP Anywhere 17
entry.content: Where can we find the error logs for SQL SAP Anywhere 17 ?

entry.title: Re: RAP Unmanaged Query with MANAGED BO Problems
entry.content: Thank you so much for your response. If so, I have a question. That's why I decided to use custom entities, not CDSs. I would like to INSERT certain data in another CBO table immediately after the data is queried through CDS. Is there a HOOK, or way to solve this in CDS and managed scenarios?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Key User Extensibility powered by Embedded Steampunk: Custom Field with ABAP implem
entry.content:
I tried this to get the value help also in SAP GUI. Unfortunately it ended up with a dump.

If I try it with a cds entity it works. With custom cds view I get a dump.

Can you confirm, that this solution should also work with key user fields that are used in SAP GUI?

best regards

Jürgen

entry.title: Re: Design of the behavior handler - ABAP RESTful Program...
entry.content:
You can use seperate classes for each entity instead of using one class for the whole RAP business object. 

What might be an option is to factor out code such that you call classes in your local class that perform certain parts of your validations / determinations.

entry.title: Re: RAP(RestfulABAP) custom entity Projection view Problems
entry.content: No, this is not possible. Custom entities do not support projections on top.

entry.title: New fields (most likely on infotype 0077) to address 2025 Medicare Reporting
entry.content:
Hello - 

Apparently there are a few new employee fields required to support new 2025 Medicare reporting for those employees participating in the associated benefits.  Has anyone heard any news of updates to the SAP ECC infotypes to accommodate these fields?

Some examples are:

- sex assigned at birth
- gender identity 

I have attached the government form for further information on the requirements.

entry.title: Re: RAP Framework - Custom Entities GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET
entry.content:
@junwu is correct.

See my answer here: 

Implementation of GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET in SAP RA... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build process Automation - How to execute automations with recorded screens in Background mode
entry.content:
Hello Experts,@Archana , @botazhat , @Chaitanya_Priya ,

We have a business use case to run the BPA automations to run in unattended mode which also in a background mode ( similar to Background jobs in SAP).

We have created an automation using screen capture option and recorded the Fiori app screens for asset distribution rules( App id : AIBU) on S4 HANA public cloud system, and we have created an process automation to read the data from a file and pass the data into this automation so that the bot can copy the data from file to the app AIBU and post the data in S4 system.

The automation is working fine end to end but its getting executed in fore ground in User machine on a browser window.

The question here is is it possible to schedule this automation as a background job in unattended mode so that this Job can run every day at given frequency and post the data in S4 system in AIBU application.

System details:

S4 HANA public cloud 2402

BPA desktop agent version: 3.24.54

Thanks a lot in advance.

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Business Technology Platform SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Counter Base Strategy Maintenance Plan
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Can we generate multiple Notification based on the Maintenance Package thru standard SAP?

For Ex: 

1.2000 Hours maintenance - Plan should generate 1 Notification, generated Order thru Notification will contain operations of 2000 Milestones.

2.4000 Hours Maintenance - Plan should generate 2 Notification (2000,4000), generated Order thru Notification will contain operations of 2000 and 400Milestones.

3.8000 Hours Maintenance - Plan should generate 3 Notification (2000,4000,8000), generated Order thru Notification will contain operations of 2000, 4000, 8000Milestones.

Or any Exit available to fulfill this requirement.

Please Help Me.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management 

SAP Asset Strategy and Performance Management

entry.title: Re: IFlow for local SQL database server integrating using cloud connector
entry.content: Do we need SAP CPI to do this? @Dushyant_Garg

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content:
Found a blog that suggested adding:

    "features": {
      "in_memory_db": true
    }

To the package.json and 

 - cp -r db/data gen/srv/srv/data

To mta.yaml. So that ...odata/v4/northbreeze/Products now displayed data and then, for some reason, had to rework the code to northbreeze.Products. Eventually:

A challenge indeed. Many thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: move away from those IDoc messages!
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I‘d like to make an addition to my above post. As we are facing first S4 installations having AIF in use for Standard APIs. As of my current understanding SAP will develop AIF in future, to provide additional Funktionalität for any kind of api. Anyways Idoc and AIF are not excluding themselves, but business has to decide which is Best for which task.

 

KR

Patrick

entry.title: The Power of Generative AI in Customer Experience Software Solutions: A Glimpse into the future
entry.content:
I'm an eighties kid. I loved my tech back then. My walkman, my Atari, my first dual tape deck with autoreverse... even my dad's hot new Betamax VCR. What fun. I never thought I'd look into the future and see what is going on today. And software? What was that? I never thought my big brother's Apple IIe computer would amount to much. Here I am today, 15 years into working with SAP, and always on the cusp of the hottest customer experience (CX) technologies. And of course the evolution of CX software is set to be profoundly influenced by generative AI which is poised to revolutionize the way businesses interact with customers, offering unprecedented levels of personalization, efficiency, and innovation. In this blog, we will explore the importance of generative AI in CX software solutions and how it will shape the industry over the next ten years.

1. Personalized Customer Interactions

 

Personalized Experiences

Generative AI has the potential to elevate personalization to new heights. By analyzing vast amounts of customer data, generative AI can create highly tailored content and interactions that resonate with individual preferences and behaviors. This level of personalization extends beyond simple product recommendations, encompassing personalized marketing messages, dynamic website content, and customized customer service interactions.

2. Enhanced Customer Support

One of the most immediate and impactful applications of generative AI is in customer support. AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants are becoming increasingly sophisticated, capable of understanding and responding to complex customer inquiries in real-time. Over the next decade, these tools will continue to improve, offering more accurate and empathetic responses, and generating routine to complex tasks, thereby enhancing the overall customer support experience and reducing the burden on human agents.

3. Predictive Analytics and Proactive Engagement

Generative AI can analyze customer data to identify patterns and predict future behaviors. This capability allows businesses to engage with customers proactively, addressing potential issues before they arise and offering relevant suggestions at the right time. Predictive analytics can also help in optimizing marketing strategies, improving customer retention, and increasing lifetime value.

4. Automated Content Creation

Creating engaging content is a time-consuming process. Generative AI can automate content creation, generating everything from product descriptions to marketing copy, blog posts, and social media updates. This not only saves time and resources but also ensures that content is consistent, relevant, and tailored to specific audience segments.

5. Improved Customer Insights

Generative AI can process and analyze large datasets much faster than traditional methods, providing deeper and more actionable customer insights. These insights can inform strategic decisions, helping businesses to better understand their customers, refine their products and services, and stay ahead of market trends.

6. Seamless Omnichannel Experiences

Customers today expect a seamless experience across all touchpoints, whether online, in-store, or through mobile apps. Generative AI can help create a unified and consistent customer journey by integrating data and interactions across different channels. This ensures that customers receive a cohesive and personalized experience, no matter how they choose to engage with a brand.

7. Dynamic Pricing and Offers

Generative AI can analyze market conditions, competitor pricing, and customer behavior to dynamically adjust pricing and offers in real-time. This ensures that businesses remain competitive while maximizing profitability and customer satisfaction. Personalized discounts and promotions based on individual buying patterns can also drive sales and foster customer loyalty.

8. Innovation in Product Development

By analyzing customer feedback and market trends, generative AI can help businesses identify opportunities for new products and services. This data-driven approach to innovation ensures that product development is aligned with customer needs and preferences, reducing the risk of product failures and enhancing market success.

9. Enhanced Security and Fraud Detection

Customer trust is paramount, and generative AI can play a crucial role in ensuring data security and fraud detection. AI algorithms can monitor and analyze transactions in real-time, identifying suspicious activities and preventing fraud before it impacts customers. This not only protects customers but also enhances their trust in the brand.

 

secure connections

10. Sustainable Business Practices

Generative AI can also contribute to sustainability efforts by optimizing resource usage, reducing waste, and improving supply chain efficiency. This aligns with the growing consumer demand for environmentally responsible businesses and can enhance brand reputation and customer loyalty.

Conclusion

The integration of generative AI into customer experience software solutions is set to transform the industry over the next decade. By offering unparalleled levels of personalization, efficiency, and innovation, generative AI will enable businesses to deliver exceptional customer experiences, drive growth, and stay ahead in an increasingly competitive market. Embracing this technology is not just an option but a necessity for businesses that aspire to thrive in the future.

As we look forward to the next ten years, the potential of generative AI in shaping the future of customer experience is immense. Now is the time for businesses to invest in this transformative technology and harness its power to create a better, more personalized, and efficient customer journey.

entry.title: How do I test a Role
entry.content:
Hello,

As a former Microsoft BI guy I know that it was quite easy to test out your security, as well for objects as for RLS by selecting another role (of course if you had priviliges).

Is something similar possible in DS.

We have quite some different profiles and I could create a Test-user and adjust the roles for that user when we need to test something. But just creating a Test-user in our environment is quite some administration and a monthly cost involved too.

So maybe there is another solution ...

entry.title: Re: Substituir lista de condição de pagamentos SAP Busine...

entry.title: Re: Signavio Process Manager: RACI Matrix, Role Management and pointers about "A", "C

Hi All,

I need to Download SAP NWDS 7.5 but I am unable to download due to not having Download authorizations.

entry.title: Re: Why can't I select Hierarchy in the Preview
entry.content:
found it, I alwaus created the Associations with the GUI (Drag & Drop), but then you ceate a normal assosiation. By doing it by hand (Texttual) then you can choose the correct type of Association !!

Silly newbie error !

entry.title: Re: Transport AVC config from DEV to PRD
entry.content: Hi Pablo, please check out various ALE Transaction to transfer AVC object one by one. e.g. BD91 for char, CLD1 for config profile, CLD3 and CLD4 for variant table, CLD2 /CUK2 for dep and dep-net, BD92 for class, BD10 for Materials etc.

entry.title: Can you capitalise an asset with depreciation?
entry.content:
Hi asset expert

Is there a way to specify accumulated depreciation at the point of capitalising an asset?

My client disposed of an asset in prior years and has recently discovered it. They can't reverse the disposal (due to a number of closed financial years) but need to take on the asset with its accumulated depreciation.

I have capitalised the asset via ABZON (without issue) but doing a write-up of depreciation (tcode ABZU) returns the error AA402 (You cannot post write ups. None of the areas to be posted in accordance with the transaction type entered manages one of the depreciation types entered or cumulative values).

Posting manual depreciation via ABMA results in "unplanned depreciation" which is gets mixed up impairments which are different to the starting depreciation.

Can I use AS91 to take on the asset with accumulated depreciation? The Accm.Ord.Deprec field is greyed/read-only. 

Any more ideas?
Thanks!

entry.title: MDK SAP - Styling Dinamically / Variables
entry.content:
Hello SAP people!

I have already asked a question and they answered it yesterday, which has helped me a lot to continue. I am learning MDK and I would like to understand one thing that I am not very clear about variables... On the main page I have a list of charges from a client that I previously select, which do not have any style applied. My application works in offline mode, so if there is no Internet connection when I try to send the request to SAP to collect the selected charges, these selected charges have to appear in a different color, that is: it has to be updated automatically after the connection error. I am not being able to do it. Please anyone can help me a little bit?

I attach a piece of code:

Collections.page:

{
"Controls": [
{
"FilterFeedbackBar": {
"ShowAllFilters": false,
"_Type": "Control.Type.FilterFeedbackBar"
},
"_Type": "Control.Type.SectionedTable",
"_Name": "SectionedTable0",
"Sections": [
{

"Controls": [

{
"_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.Button",
"_Name": "FormCellButton0",
"IsVisible": true,
"Separator": true,
"Title": "$(L,'collect')",
"TextAlignment": "Center",
"ButtonType": "{ButtonType/Text}",
"Enabled": true,
"OnPress": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/OnPressCollect.js"
}

]

},

{
"Separators": {
"TopSectionSeparator": false,
"BottomSectionSeparator": true,
"HeaderSeparator": true,
"FooterSeparator": true,
"ControlSeparator": true
},
"_Type": "Section.Type.ObjectTable",
"Target": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/GetCustomerUnpaidDocs.js",
"_Name": "SectionObjectTable0",
"Visible": true,
"EmptySection": {
"FooterVisible": false
},
"ObjectCell": {
"ContextMenu": {
"Items": [],
"LeadingItems": [],
"PerformFirstActionWithFullSwipe": true,
"TrailingItems": []
},
"Title": "{KunnrName1} ({Kunnr})",
"Subhead": "$(L,'rep'): {Salesrep} ({Compcode}) $(L,'payer'): {Filkd}",
"Description": "$(L,'inv_n'): {Invoice} $(L,'inv_f') $(D,{Bldat},'','',{format:'short'}) $(L,'doc'): {Docnumber}/{Buzei} {Year}",
"StatusText": "$(C,{Dueamount}, {Waers} )",
"SubstatusText": "$(L,'due'): $(D,{Duedate})",
"PreserveIconStackSpacing": false,
"AccessoryType": "Checkmark",
"Styles": {
"Title": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/SetStyle.js",
"Subhead": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/SetStyle.js",
"Description": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/SetStyle.js",
"StatusText": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/SetStyle.js",
"SubstatusText": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/SetStyle.js"
},
"Selected": false
},
"HighlightSelectedItem": true,
"Selection": {
"ExitOnLastDeselect": false,
"LongPressToEnable": "None",
"Mode": "Multiple"
},
"OnSelectionChanged": "/CollectionsApp/Rules/Collections/OnSelectionChanged.js"
}

]

}

OnSelectionChanged.js:

There is this line that saves an array of those selected collections and their data: 

CommonLibrary.setStateVariable(context, 'SelectedBindingCollections', selectedBindingCollections, 'Collections');

And obviously, in SetStyle.js inside the ObjectCell are the conditions with the .less that I want to apply when is 'PendingOperation', that is in the level of the 'Compcode', 'Doctype',.. etc etc, basically the normal client data.

Well this is all and I don't know if I explained well to understand... thanks!! 😄

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA BW Query usage statistics
entry.content:
Hi guys

With the help of SAP Help Documentation and the following video I get an idea of how the new statitisc work in BW/4HANA Cockpit.

I hope it is helpful to someone else

Oscar A.

entry.title: Supported form outputs
entry.content:
We currently run ECC 6.0 EHP 6 with many SAPScripts - we were going to venture into conversion to Smartform, however we encountered issues in the purchasing module and being told (by SAP Support/Ask an Expert) that purchasing doesn't support Smartform and Adobe would be needed.

We plan to move to ECC 6.0 EHP 8 next year. 

Question: When we move to EHP8, does purchasing support Smartforms? 

Question: What is Best Practice for output? Smartform, Adobe?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Supported form outputs
entry.content: As of now support Smartforms is supported by SAP in standard

entry.title: Re: SAP NWDS 7.5 Download
entry.content: why you don't have authorization?

entry.title: Re: Error MLCCS 202 in VFX3
entry.content: Hello, The solution was to open an incident with SAP, which sent us the program ZMLHELP_CCS_LOTSIZE_ADJUST to create in our environment and for SAP to perform the adjustment in PRD.

entry.title: Re: Planning Object Creation and Maintenance in SAP IBP - Part 1
entry.content:
HI @sumaghosh ,

Creation / deletion of planning objects options in Excel UI is controlled through different authorization. Please check SAP IBP help for more details.

entry.title: Candidate Profile Override
entry.content:
Hello guys, 
Is it possible to create an override in the Candidate profile? I need to configure the Gender field for all countries, but it should not be enabled in the US. The field should be mandatory for all other countries except the US.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a...
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

The below listed script would help to fetch the variable values that are selected part of back end variable "Zxxxxxxx".

Here this variable "Zxxxxxxx"  is being used in Story and has some values already selected.

In my case two plants. The O/P shown is fetched two values that are selected in the variable.

var sel = Table_2.getDataSource().getVariableValues("Zxxxxxxx");
console.log(sel);

Thanks,

Venkat

entry.title: make sure that your IP address is configured for connections to SAP HANA Cloud. Or allow connections
entry.content:
With Refrecene to the blog/Turorial -  https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cap-service-deploy.html

Step 3 Deploy using cf deploy  then Step 2.  error in log using command cf deploy gen/mta.tar.

Blog suggests  the below. 

If your deployment fails during deploy of my-bookshop-db-deployer, make sure that your IP address is configured for connections to SAP HANA Cloud. Or allow connections to all IP addresses at your own risk. We recommend to revert that setting after you’ve completed the tutorial.

How to alllow IP address that is configured for connections to SAP HANA Cloud. Or how and where to allow connections to all IP addresses at your own risk. ( on Mac).

entry.title: Adding Attachments to Emails in SAP CPI Using Groovy
entry.content:
In the world of integration, sending emails with attachments is a common requirement. For SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) developers, using Groovy scripts can streamline this process, ensuring that files are sent without corruption. In this blog, we will explore how to convert a message body to a Base64 format and use it as an attachment in an email, all while avoiding common pitfalls that can lead to file corruption.

In a previous blog, I explored how to password protect a PDF file using Groovy. I invite you to check it out, and please feel free to share any questions or feedback you may have. I would be delighted to assist you

The Complete Groovy Script

Here’s the complete Groovy script that we will dissect:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.apache.camel.impl.DefaultAttachment
import javax.mail.util.ByteArrayDataSource

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String) as String;
    
    // 1: Get the binary attachment content in the form of a byte array
    def pdfBytes = body.decodeBase64()
    
    // 2: Construct a ByteArrayDataSource object with the byte array and the content's MIME type
    def data = new ByteArrayDataSource(pdfBytes, 'application/pdf')
    
    // 3: Construct a DefaultAttachment object
    def att = new DefaultAttachment(data)
    
    // 4: Add the attachment to the message
    message.addAttachmentObject('SamplePdf.pdf', att)
    
    return message
}

 

Step-by-Step Explanation

Step 1: Import Necessary Classes

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.apache.camel.impl.DefaultAttachment
import javax.mail.util.ByteArrayDataSource

 

In this initial step, we import the necessary classes. The Message class is crucial for handling the message content, while DefaultAttachment and ByteArrayDataSource are used to create and manage the email attachment.

Step 2: Retrieve the Message Body

 

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String) as String;

 

Here, we define the processData method, which is the entry point for our Groovy script. It takes a Message object as a parameter, allowing us to manipulate the message's content and attachments. In the next line we retrieve the body of the message, which is expected to be in Base64 format. This is done by calling getBody on the message object and casting it to a String.

Step 3: Decode the Base64 Content

 

def pdfBytes = body.decodeBase64()

 

In this step, we decode the Base64-encoded string into a byte array. This is crucial because attachments need to be in binary format, and decoding ensures that we have the correct byte representation of the file.

Step 4: Create a ByteArrayDataSource

 

def data = new ByteArrayDataSource(pdfBytes, 'application/pdf')

 

We create a ByteArrayDataSource object, which takes the byte array and the MIME type of the content (in this case, application/pdf). This object acts as a bridge between the byte data and the email attachment.

Step 5: Construct the Attachment and add attachment to the message

 

def att = new DefaultAttachment(data)
message.addAttachmentObject('SamplePdf.pdf', att)

 

Here, we construct a DefaultAttachment object using the ByteArrayDataSource. This object encapsulates the attachment data and is ready to be added to the email. Finally, we add the attachment to the message using addAttachmentObject. We specify the name of the file as it will appear in the email (SamplePdf.pdf) and pass the attachment object.

Conclusion

Using Groovy scripts in SAP CPI to add attachments to emails can significantly enhance the functionality of your integration flows. By following the steps outlined in this blog, you can ensure that files are sent without corruption, providing a seamless experience for end-users. Whether you are sending reports, invoices, or any other documents, this method will help you achieve your goals efficiently.

Feel free to experiment with different file types and MIME types to suit your specific needs! Let me know if you need any further adjustments!

entry.title: Regarding SF Learning Admin - is there a way to deactivated multiple instructor accounts at once?
entry.content: My organization is interested in the ability to deactivate multiple instructor accounts on the SuccessFactors Learning Administration side of things without having to search for and edit individual user accounts an unchecking the "Active" checkbox. Is this possible? We have nearly 2000 accounts that we'd like to make inactive.  Would love to not have to go into each, one by one.  Thanks!

entry.content: Thank you so much for this excellent article ! It saved my **bleep** today 🙂

entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have third party ATS tool(ICIMS) new hires syncing to EC and we have written some business rule for new hires to auto populate the fields which are not coming from the ICIMS tool however it is working when we hire new employee manually in EC.

Please suggest how to assign business rule and what is the workaround.

Regards

ST

entry.title: Re: Generating PDF on BTP CF using CAP and Puppeteer

entry.title: Connecting SAP BW/4HANA to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear SAP Datasphere Community,

I'm having some trouble connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP BW/4HANA and could really use some guidance.

Here's what's happening:

I logged into Semantic Onboarding and tried to set up an SAP BW/4HANA connection.

I got this message:
"You must have access to an existing space with at least one relevant connection before you can import entities from it."

I tried again through the Connections menu and selected "SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer."

This time, it asked for:
Live Data Connection
SAP HANA SQL ACCESS

What I need help with:

How can I make sure I have access to an existing space with the relevant connection?
What are the steps to set up the "Live Data Connection" and "SAP HANA SQL ACCESS"?

Any detailed steps or advice would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Priyanka

entry.title: Wrong posting date for sequential order
entry.content:
Hello dear Experts,

Client needs the ability to create billing with a backdated date, for example: today is 24.07 and the client wants to create billing with billing date of 18.07. In their system, when I try to release document for accounting that meets the requirements, the fallowing message appears " Wrong posting date for sequential order (Header Phase 001)". Do you have anny ideas how I can reslove this problem?

Thanks in advance experts.

I understand why we don't delete them but having every single location we've ever had come up on a list is rather frustrating to scroll through.

entry.title: Re: GUI 8.0 for Windows 10 and 11
entry.content: Recently I got new windows 11 laptop. I installed SAP GUI 8.0 patch 6. SAP Logon works for a while then it crashes. I searched the Sap support portal with no success. Please advise

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One upgrade path - upgraded!
entry.content:
Hi @Guy_Sujetzki 

This means a great saving of time and effort that every SAP Business One professional will appreciate.

Kind regards,

 Erick

entry.title: Re: MDK SAP - Styling Dinamically / Variables
entry.content:
I'm not sure I understand what you are wanting to accomplish. Are you using the Object Table in multi-select mode to pick from the list the items you want to process in the backend?  Once you pick them what happens?  In an offline app you would normally update those entities locally and when you have a connection sync to the backend but it depends on your app / backend / requirements of course.

Your rules for the styles just need to return the name of the style class from the .less file you want applied to the cell.  Your rule can use whatever logic you need to determine if the cell should be styled or not.

entry.title: Re: Can't found app "Manage Production Supply Areas"
entry.content:
Hi @Pdud_97 

 

The app is available with the assignment of the business catalog SAP_SCM_BC_KNBN_CTRLC_MGMT_MC 

 

Please check in the Business Catalog app if SAP_SCM_BC_KNBN_CTRLC_MGMT_MC appears

 

If not , then you need to active one of the scope items below

 

1E3 Material Replenishment with Kanban - External Procurement
4B3 Material Replenishment with Kanban - In-House Production
4B4 Material Replenishment with Kanban - Stock Transfer

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking to see if anyone can give me guidance on the landed cost functionality. I have watched many YouTube videos and read many articles. The one thing I cannot tell is if this feature is customizable or not. From what I have seen then the standard use case will not work for what I am trying to do. 

I am trying to apply a standard freight costs to my incoming raw material through the GRPO with a journal entry of:

Debit - Raw Material
Credit - Standard Raw Material
Credit - Standard Freight 

Is landed cost the right function for this set up

Any advice would be helpful

entry.title: Signavio Security
entry.content:
Hello, please I need to confirm if Signavio meets the following requirements on its platform:

- Data encryption is compliant with FIPS 140 level 2 or higher?
- Does the platform have implemented Data Loss Prevention (DLP) mechanisms?
- Does it have mechanisms to prevent Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks (Anti-DDoS)?
- Does it allow its clients to conduct independent vulnerability assessments?

Tanks in advance

Kind Regards

Gustavo Giusto

entry.title: SAP version 1809 on S/4HANA to SAP RISE
entry.content:
Hello architects,

I have a customer with SAP version 1809 on S/4HANA and the customer does not want to upgrade before going to RISE.

I have identified that SAP has created note 3246630 for these scenarios and I would like to understand if it is useful for this customer ?

The customer is in Onpremise and will go to RISE.

entry.title: Transform your phone’s camera into a next-gen input tool
entry.content:
The Scenario

Imagine you work at a busy international airport where shipments from global suppliers arrive daily. Recently, your country introduced a regulation requiring all airports to maintain a list of international suppliers using air freight to send parcels smaller than 1 m³. Your task is to extract supplier information directly from shipping documents such as invoices, packing lists, and courier bills, and record this information in an S4/HANA system set up by the national department of foreign trade.

 

Why Pen and Paper Won’t Cut It

Managing shipment details manually by noting them down on a piece of paper and digitizing your notes later is not just time-consuming but feels like something out of the second century AD! While creating a mobile app could eliminate the need for manual data entry, typing on a small screen can be cumbersome and prone to errors, especially with special characters often found in foreign languages.

 

A Cutting-Edge Solution

SAP Build Code on SAP Discovery Center

 

SAP Document Information Extraction Service on SAP Discovery Center

 

Instead, you can build a cutting-edge solution using SAP Build Code and SAP Document Information Extraction Service to build an application that lets you extract information from images. Let’s see how you can build this amazing experience.

 

Building the solution

Getting Started: Essential Tutorials

Although this blog won’t be a step-by-step tutorial, it will point you toward the essential resources and concepts to help you get started.

  • Complete the Tutorials:
    • Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents
    • Try Out the SAP BTP SDK Wizard for Android
      Note: You can also implement this mobile app using SAP BTP SDK for iOS or Mobile Development Kit (MDK).
 
Create  Integration with SAP Mobile Services

You can either use your own use-case or this supplier form. Based on your use-case, you need to create a schema on the SAP Document Information Extraction Service, similar to the example below. If you are using the provided supplier form, select "Invoice" as the Document Type when creating the schema. Adding a description helps the AI model contextually match the extracted results to the fields defined in the schema.

 
Seamless Integration with SAP Mobile Services

To safeguard your application from potential disruptions due to API changes, add your APIs to SAP Mobile Services instead of hardcoding them into your app.

  • Retrieve API Credentials:
    • In the Instances & Subscriptions section of your sub-account, locate the service instance for Document Information Extraction Service (e.g., default_document-information-extraction-trial).
    • Click on the key under credentials and note the following:
      • url
      • uaa → clientid
      • uaa → clientsecret
      • uaa → url

Note: If a key does not exist, you can create one.

  • Configure SAP Mobile Services:
    • Go to SAP Mobile Services and create a new destination in the Mobile Services Connectivity.

      Step

      Field Key

      Field Value

      Basic Info

      Destination Name

      dox.service.destination

      Basic Info

      URL

      <url>/document-information-extraction/v1/

      Destination Configuration

      SSO Mechanism

      OAuth2 Client Credentials

      Destination Configuration

      Token Service URL

      <uaa → url>/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials&response_type=token

      Destination Configuration

      Client ID

      <uaa → clientid>

      Destination Configuration

      Client Secret

      <uaa → clientsecret>

    • Test your configuration by using the “ping” and “launch in browser” actions for the newly added destination. You should see a blank page upon successful authentication.
      Note: If prompted, please provide valid credentials to authenticate yourself.
Building the experience in your app
  • Write code to capture an image and send it to the Document Information Extraction Service using this API: Upload Document API.
  • Write code to pre-populate an input form from results extracted from the uploaded image using this API: Get Result API.

For inspiration, please check out the Scan2InputPoC repository on GitHub.
Note: this project is a proof of concept created by the author and is not an official recommendation from SAP; it is provided as-is.

 

Testing the solution

 

 

 

 

 

Once you have built your application with the necessary logic and configuration, it's time for the fun part: testing it in different scenarios such as:

  • Lighting Conditions: Test in various lighting settings such as dim lighting, sunlight, and artificial light.
  • Document Angles: Position the document at different angles on a flat surface or vertically.
  • Special Characters: Test documents containing special characters not found in the English alphabet.
  • Handwritten Documents: Try scanning and processing handwritten documents to evaluate the application’s accuracy.

Here's a video demo of what the capability would look like: 

 

What Next?

As you can see, you can quickly build a solution using SAP BTP to address a demanding problem in an ever-changing world. This powerful technology can also be used for digitizing patient records in healthcare and capturing maintenance reports in field services. I am very eager for all of you to try this out and hear about your possible use cases in the comments section.

 

Important Links

  • Product Help: SAP Build Code | SAP Mobile Services | SAP Document Information Extraction Service
  • Get Started Tutorials: SAP BTP SDK for Android | SAP Document Information Extraction Service
  • API Reference: SAP Document Information Extraction Service | SAP BTP SDK for Android

entry.title: Re: How can I Copy Material Master in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Arghadip

 

Product Master records can be copied in Manage Product Master Data app but Masking Material Description is not possible . If you want to create materials automatically then you can create your own custom app

using API_PRODUCT_SRV and populate the description with whatever logic / format you wish

 

Best regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Guide: Deploying CAP Application with SQLite Database
entry.content: Hi Jay, I managed to include the db.sqlite in the MTA archive, but still no success in the application. I still get: SqliteError: no such table

entry.title: HANA Cloud database connection not present in run configuration dependencies
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'm developing a full-stack CAP application that includes three main components: a Fiori list page for front end, and a SAP HANA Cloud database for data storage, and a CAP app to tie the two together.

I have successfully bound and deployed to the database connection, as evidenced by the schema's produced and my ability to import data via the database explorer. My understanding is that the action of binding includes the database as a "dependency" to the project.


My issue is that I cannot seem to expose database data to the Fiori list page. I haven't started developing the list page yet, but this data should at the very least be listed in the raw OData output when I run the application. The connected database is not showing up as a dependency under run configurations, and I do not have the option to make a "production" configuration to see if it's any different.

Unsurprisingly, running this configuration uses the in-memory database feature, and doesn't expose the HANA Cloud data I've set up.

Could someone tell me where my issue could be? I've tried researching this question, but everyone seems to assume that if you set up your database connection properly, it should be available in run configurations. No one seems to be able to do the first but not the second.

Lastly, I'll include two files from the project I've been messing with the most. I assume the problem is somewhere in "[project root]/package.json" or "[project root]/mta.yaml", but again I can't be sure.

package.json:

{
  "name": "BWZ_User_Statistics",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A simple CAP project.",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1",
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4",
    "express": "^4"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.6",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1",
    "@sap/cds-dk": ">=7"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve"
  },
  "cds": {
    "build": {
      "target": "gen",
      "tasks": [
        {
          "for": "hana",
          "src": "db",
          "options": {
            "model": [
              "db",
              "srv",
              "app"
            ]
          }
        },
        {
          "for": "nodejs",
          "src": "srv",
          "options": {
            "model": [
              "db",
              "srv",
              "app"
            ]
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "requires": {
      "[production]": {
        "db": "hana",
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      }
    }
  }
}

mta.yaml:

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: BWZ_User_Statistics
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    requires:
      - name: cross-container-service-2
      - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-auth
      # - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-db
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
      readiness-health-check-type: http
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
  - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    requires:
      # - name: cross-container-service-1
      #   properties:
      #     TARGET_CONTAINER: ~{hdi-container-name}
      - name: cross-container-service-2
        # group: SERVICE_REPLACEMENTS
        properties:
          # key: ServiceName_1
          # service: ~{the-service-name}
          TARGET_CONTAINER: ~{the-service-name}
      # - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  - name: BWZ_User_Statistics
    type: approuter.nodejs
    path: app/router
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
        group: destinations
        properties:
          forwardAuthToken: true
          name: srv-api
          url: ~{srv-url}
      - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-auth
    parameters:
      disk-quota: 256M
      keep-existing-routes: true
      memory: 256M
resources:
  # - name: cross-container-service-1
  #   type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
  #   parameters:
  #     service-name: hditest
  #   properties:
  #     hdi-container-name: ${service-name}
  - name: cross-container-service-2
    type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
    parameters:
      service-name: test_1
    properties:
      the-service-name: ${service-name}
  - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
      path: ./xs-security.json
      config:
        xsappname: BWZ_User_Statistics-${org}-${space}
        tenant-mode: dedicated
  # - name: BWZ_User_Statistics-db
  #   type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
  #   parameters:
  #     service: hana
  #     service-plan: hdi-shared
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: IDOC Data Records Searchable Fields in WE02
entry.content:
Where can we maintain or configure the ability to search fields visible in data records detail of transaction WE02.  These are fields listed in the "Content of Selected Segment", two columns are "Fld Name" and "Fld Cont." (contains the data we want to search on.)

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Performance Issue After Converting SAC Stories to Opt...
entry.content:
Hello,
Thank you for sharing your experience. We are primarily using simple stories without any scripts.

entry.title: Re: Task 6 - API endpoint with payload required (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs"
entry.content: Hi there - I wrote this up - let me know what you think! "Automatic validation in OData and REST calls with CAP" https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2024/07/24/automatic-validation-in-odata-and-rest-calls-with-cap/

entry.title: Re: Deploying CAP services with the in-memory SQLite database
entry.content:
Thank you @zavadav . Adding the default sqlite3 dependency was the final step to make this work.

I left both @cap-js/sqlite and sqlite3 and it worked.

entry.title: Re: MDK SAP - Styling Dinamically / Variables
entry.content: What I mean is that I want to change the state of a variable, in this case ‘PendingOperation’ to false, because what I want to do is to update the style of the title and other texts, but I can't get the style to be executed again. I've been looking around and there is a .redraw() but it doesn't work because it goes to the initial state (I think) and the style change is not reflected. I'm used to working in React and Angular and components communicate with each other by using variables in a service, observables or parameters of this style.So I'm looking for something similar: that the styles.js learns about the changes and returns the appropriate .less class.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: move away from those IDoc messages!
entry.content:
@pazabel WLF_IDOC was a major  game changer when it comes to managing daily business IDoc traffic - totally agree!

against SAP Standard: Never change a running system!

#idoc #loveit #asyncbutalmostthere

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content:
@gphadnis2000 Did you see this?

Solved: cf push error "Routes cannot be mapped to destinat... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content:
Hello @qmacro ,

My submission for task 7 :

Odata Call:

 

CDS REPL was a great help to understand the querying with different options. Also your blog Turning an OData expand into a cds.ql CQL query with a projection function in CAP was a great help too to clear the concepts. Thanks a ton!
